body:
## Summary

- Require spaced repetition due timestamps to be canonical UTC ISO strings before due checks or due sorting accept them.
- Add validator parity coverage for malformed dueAt values and malformed now values that JavaScript Date parsing would otherwise reinterpret.
- Extend focused runtime tests and SETUP journal handoff for SPACED-REPETITION-DUE-TIMESTAMP-CANONICAL-GUARD-1.

## Root Cause

`isDue` and `sortByDueAscending` used loose Date parsing, so date-only strings, offset timestamps, and rollover dates could be interpreted as valid due times even though the project already defines a stricter canonical timestamp contract.

## Validation

- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --test tests/content-spaced-repetition-schema.test.js`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --test --test-name-pattern 'isDue|sortByDueAscending|dueCards|dueCount|rollover|date-only|timezone|spaced repetition' tests/v1-1-pro-foundations.test.js tests/content-spaced-repetition-schema.test.js tests/v1-1-review-store.test.js`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node scripts/validate-content.js --focus-spaced-repetition-schema`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npm run validate:content`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npm run typecheck -- --pretty false`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npm run lint`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npm run test:ownership`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npx --no-install prettier --check lib/learning/spacedRepetition.ts scripts/validate-content.js tests/content-spaced-repetition-schema.test.js tests/v1-1-pro-foundations.test.js tests/v1-1-review-store.test.js`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`

Note: the broader suggested pattern containing `canonical` also selects unrelated Pro pricing/paywall tests that are already red on current main (`29 kr` vs `29 SEK`, and missing `REMOVE_ADS_PRICE_LABEL`). The spaced-repetition-specific pattern above passed.

No Vercel CLI was run.
title: [codex] setup: require canonical spaced repetition due timestamps
